user_query:
I’m Det. Thabo Mokoena at Johannesburg Central. Before we image a suspect’s laptop, I need a verifiable snapshot of this workstation’s current health to attach to my case notes.

Input:
- Read metadata from input/case_metadata.json and use it in the report header and machine-readable summary.

Requirements:
1) Collect a cross-platform system status snapshot by attempting a reasonable set of OS-appropriate commands and capturing both successes and failures. At minimum, attempt these (or their closest equivalents on the detected OS):
   - OS/host: uname -srm (Unix/macOS), systeminfo or wmic os get Caption,Version (Windows)
   - Uptime/last boot: uptime (Unix/macOS), wmic os get LastBootUpTime (Windows)
   - CPU: lscpu (Linux), sysctl -n machdep.cpu.brand_string (macOS), wmic cpu get Name,NumberOfCores,NumberOfLogicalProcessors (Windows)
   - Memory: free -m (Linux), vm_stat (macOS), wmic OS get TotalVisibleMemorySize,FreePhysicalMemory (Windows)
   - Disks: df -h (Unix/macOS), wmic logicaldisk get Size,FreeSpace,Caption (Windows)
   - Network interfaces: ip -o -4 addr or ifconfig (Unix), ipconfig (Windows)
   - Listening ports: ss -tulwn (Linux), lsof -nP -iTCP -sTCP:LISTEN (macOS), netstat -ano (Windows)
   You may try multiple alternatives where appropriate; include the first one that succeeds. For any command that fails, capture the non-zero exit code and the first line of stderr.

2) Internet connectivity and integrity check:
   - Download the official CA certificate bundle file named "cacert.pem" published by the cURL project (from the cURL project’s official domain) and save it to artifacts/external/cacert.pem. Do not hardcode a mirror; obtain it from the official cURL site.
   - Compute its SHA-256 hash and write the hex digest to artifacts/external/cacert.sha256 (single line containing only the hex). Also record the file size in bytes.
   - If the download fails, record the error in the logs and mark the connectivity check as failed in the report.

3) Produce these outputs under the workspace:
   - artifacts/raw/commands.jsonl: one JSON object per attempted command with at least: timestamp (ISO 8601), command (string exactly as executed), exit_code (int), stdout (string, may be truncated), stderr (string, may be truncated).
   - artifacts/report/system_status_summary.json: a machine-readable summary with fields: case_number, officer_name, station, timestamp, hostname, os (string), uptime_or_last_boot (string), cpu_model (string), cpu_cores_logical (int if available), memory_total_mb and memory_available_mb (numbers if available), disks (array of objects with identifier/mount, total_gb, used_gb, percent_used if available), network_interfaces (array with name and ipv4 if available), listening_ports_count (int if derivable), top_processes_cpu and top_processes_mem (each an array of up to 5 items with process name/pid and metric if derivable), external_reference (object with file: "cacert.pem", size_bytes, sha256, source: "cURL project", status: "downloaded" or "failed"), failures (array of objects with command and error_snippet for any commands that failed). Include a readiness field set to "ready" if any writable disk shows at least 10 GB free, "needs_attention" if disks were obtained and none show >= 10 GB free, or "indeterminate" if disk info could not be derived.
   - artifacts/report/system_status_report.md: a concise, officer-facing report with:
     • Header: case_number, officer_name, station, timestamp
     • Summary paragraph: one-paragraph status update referencing the case number and stating the readiness value (ready / needs_attention / indeterminate)
     • System snapshot: hostname, OS, uptime/last boot, CPU model and logical cores, memory total/available, disk usage summary, count of listening TCP ports, brief list of active network interfaces with IPv4 addresses
     • External connectivity check: source (cURL project), target file name (cacert.pem), file size in bytes, SHA-256; or the error if it failed
     • Command execution summary: for each attempted command, show success/failure and either a key extracted metric (for successes) or the first line of stderr (for failures)

4) General constraints:
   - Use only standard OS tools and/or the Python standard library; no third-party installations.
   - Do not require administrative privileges.
   - All timestamps in outputs should be ISO 8601. Ensure paths match exactly as specified.

Deliverables to verify:
- artifacts/raw/commands.jsonl exists with multiple records including failures if any occurred.
- artifacts/external/cacert.pem and artifacts/external/cacert.sha256 exist (or commands.jsonl and the report clearly document the download failure if it could not be retrieved).
- artifacts/report/system_status_summary.json and artifacts/report/system_status_report.md exist and contain the required sections/fields and values populated from input/case_metadata.json and the collected system data. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
